notes: NT Server 4.0 used in Digital's 6,712 tpmC benchmark; evolved to Windows 2000 Server
Achieved TPC-C record: 6,712 tpmC at $65/tpmC; 4-way Pentium Pro configuration
Used in Digital's TPC-C record server; next-gen Klamath (266 MHz) and Deschutes (333 MHz) projected
Alpha clock speeds at 500 MHz reported; AlphaServer clusters exceeded 30,000 tpmC in Unix
Industry-standard OLTP benchmark measuring orders per minute; used for server market segmentation
Codename Wolfpack; shipped as MSCS in Windows NT 4.0 Enterprise Edition 1997; evolved to Windows Server Failover Clustering
Used by Sequent and others to scale NT beyond 8-way; Aberdeen noted NUMA required more Microsoft cooperation
Database used in Digital and Compaq NT TPC-C benchmark configurations
